howItWorks.intro: This noise exposure calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: OSHA Limit
rules.explanation: OSHA permissible exposure halves for every 5 dB increase above 90.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.q: What noise level causes hearing damage?
faq.a: Prolonged exposure above 85 dB can cause hearing loss. Above 120 dB causes immediate pain.
Conversation: 60 dB, lawn mower: 90 dB, rock concert: 110 dB, gunshot: 140+ dB.

howWeCalculate.intro: This noise exposure calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.
steps: Enter your specific values into the calculator fields above
